meta.purpose: Scenario signals for open-source project judgment.
meta.load_condition: Load when user input is about repo review, adoption problems, or open-source project evaluation.

scenes.name: User asks why their open-source project has no users
scenes.trigger_signal: User mentions low stars, no adoption, few downloads, 'nobody is using it', or asks for growth advice.

sub_scenarios.trap_belief: The project needs more features, better documentation, or more promotion.

three_questions.belief: Can a visitor determine within 30 seconds (a) what specific problem this solves and (b) why it is better than alternatives?
three_questions.state: Read the README first screen as a first-time visitor. Count the seconds until you understand the value proposition.
three_questions.need: A README optimized for 30-second value-entry, not comprehensive description.

sub_scenarios.action_template: Time the value-entry test. Read the README first screen and report: how many seconds until the visitor understands the problem and the alternative comparison. If > 30 seconds, this is the adoption bottleneck.
sub_scenarios.replace: Do not suggest more features, more docs, or more promotion. Fix the 30-second value-entry gap first.
sub_scenarios.expected_result: Visitor converts from 'what is this' to 'I need this' within 30 seconds.
